## Welfare {#sec-welfare}

Expected total welfare is

$$
\E[W] = \E[U_L] + \E[U_M].
$$

Therefore

$$
\E[W^{ML}] = \mu - V\kappa\nu + \frac{V^2\kappa^2}{2},
$$ {#eq-ew-ml}

$$
\E[W^{S}] = V - \mu + \frac{\mu^2 + \delta^2}{2V}.
$$ {#eq-ew-lm}

where the superscript $S$ refers to the separating LM outcome.

The sign of $\E[W^{ML}] - \E[W^{S}]$ depends jointly on
$V$, $\mu$, and $\delta$. The binary extension therefore delivers a
sharp characterization of timing incentives without forcing a unique
welfare ranking across timing regimes.
